Title: Sylas
Alias(es): The Most Dangerous Mage in the Land
Age: Current27 – 32 years old Timeline Born965 AN – 970 AN Edit [1] [2]

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
Sylas of Dregbourne was born to a poor family in Demacia, where he developed magical abilities at a young age. In accordance with the common Demacian belief that magic was an affliction, Sylas's parents convinced him to turn himself over to the Mageseekers in hopes of him living a better life. The mageseekers quickly took notice of Sylas's unique ability to sense the magic within others and exploited his gift to identify hidden mages throughout the city. Over time, Sylas began to notice a troubling hypocrisy: magic was a lot more prevalent among Demacia's elite and wealthy, some of them even being the most vocal critics of magic. Having seen the poor punished for their affliction and himself branded a criminal by Demacian society, the first seeds of doubt were planted into Sylas's mind.

Sylas and another young mage, a girl named Wisteria, were taken under the wing of a mageseeker and fellow mage, Killan, who helped them learn to control their powers. Unlike other mageseekers, Killan cared for his pupils and tried to ease their life in captivity under the mageseekers.

One day, Sylas and his Mageseeker handlers found a young mage girl in the countryside, forced to live in hiding to avoid persecution. Sylas took pity on her due to being so young so he tried to protect her from his handlers, but he accidentally brushed against her skin, causing Sylas to discover his ability to absorb magic and fire back her lightning magic uncontrollably, resulting in the deaths of the girl as well as two Mageseekers. Sylas tried to evade the law, but he was labelled as the most dangerous mage alive and when he was caught, he was sentenced to imprisonment for life.

While imprisoned, Killan cut contact with him, but Wisteria visited him out of pity and was disgusted that he couldn't control his powers.

children.title: Meeting Lux
children.textContent:
With his hands chained by petricite shackles, Sylas was imprisoned in a Mageseeker compound for fifteen years, growing disillusioned and vengeful towards the kingdom. One day, Luxanna of House Crownguard discovered him in his cell when she visited the Mageseeker prison in search of a way to control her powers. Sylas was able to quickly detect that she was a mage, causing her to flee.

Lux returned shortly after under the pretense of her work with the Illuminators, but Sylas saw through her lie and demanded to know what she truly wanted. Lux confessed that she wanted to understand how he had known she was a mage, though Sylas recognized that she also wanted to know if anyone else could detect her as well. She offered him anything to keep quiet, but Sylas refused, assuming her family was accustomed to buying their way out of any problem. Lux retorted that he knew nothing about her and, if he was truly so perceptive, he should ask himself why he was imprisoned in the dungeon at all. She accused him of being a murderer, to which Sylas replied that his only crime was being born a mage. He told her the truth of his imprisonment, how he was born with the ability to detect magic and that his parents handed him over to the mageseekers so that he might live a better life, but he had accidentally killed his handlers and a young mage when they forced him to arrest her. Lux apologized for what had happened to him, but told Sylas that he had no idea what it was like to live in constant fear, hiding who you truly were. Before she left, Sylas decided to help her learn how to control her magic and asked that she return.

When Lux came back, Sylas began teaching her how to use her abilities. He told her to stop fearing her magic and to confront it at its worst, as only then could it be conquered. In exchange, Lux brought him a book to read. Though he agreed to read it, Sylas dismissed it as propaganda, leading the two to debate their opposing views of the kingdom. As Lux's visits became more frequent, their friendship deepened. Sylas would help her practice her magic, while Lux brought him more books read. One book in particular caught Sylas's attention: the writings of the Durand architects, who had created the very shackles that kept him imprisoned. However, their private works were guarded by the mageseekers.

Lux soon informed Sylas that her family had arranged for her to be betrothed to Prince Jarvan IV, revealing that she was a Crownguard. In turn, Sylas revealed that the Crownguards were the ones responsible for his imprisonment. Seeing her guilt, Sylas convinced Lux to steal the Durands' confidential texts from the mageseekers' vault for him to read. From these writings, Sylas learned the truth about petricite: it did not nullify magic, rather, it stored it. When he returned the scroll to Lux, he touched her hand and, using his powers in conjunction with his shackles, secretly absorbed her magic. After she left, Sylas was able to channel Lux's magic and fired it at the wall.

When Lux's family discovered their meetings, her aunt Tianna arranged for Sylas to be executed the following day. As he was brought out to be beheaded, Lux forced her way through the crowd to reach him. She tried to plead with the onlookers, declaring that Sylas was human, and that Demacia's cruelty was what turned people into monsters, but they remained unmoved. The guards attempted to escort her off the stage, but Lux rushed toward Sylas and gave him a tearful goodbye hug. With this direct contact, Sylas absorbed her magic and broke free from his restraints. He unleashed her power upon the crowd, attacking mageseekers and civilians alike. Sylas then blasted open the mageseekers' prison, freeing the imprisoned mages within. He incited them into a riot and sent them out into the city. However, Sylas was stopped by Garen, Lux's brother and captain of the Dauntless Vanguard. As they clashed, Sylas's stolen magic began to fade. When he blocked a blow from Garen's blade, he discovered he could absorb its magic as well, redirecting it back at the captain. Sylas moved to finish him off, but Lux begged him to stop. Sylas relented, leaving to kill the king, and left Lux with the parting words that she should not spare her kindness to those that hated her.

children.title: The Birth of the Revolution
children.textContent:
Sylas led the freed mages to the royal palace to confront King Jarvan III, only to discover that the king had already retreated into the inner chambers. Instead, they were met by his son, Jarvan IV. The prince and his guards cornered Sylas, but he absorbed the ancient magic stored within the castle's petricite columns and unleashed it against them. Jarvan IV exchanged blows with Sylas, but Sylas drew upon dark magic from the pillars and incapacitated the prince. With Jarvan IV captured, Sylas planned to execute both father and son together. However, when the mages breached the king's chamber, they found Jarvan III and his guards already dead, but not by their hands.

Despite this, Jarvan IV blamed Sylas for his father's death. Still needing someone to make an example of, the mages dragged Jarvan IV before the city to stand trial. Sylas announced to the kingdom that Jarvan III was dead, and that his son would be tried for being complicit in his father's reign of terror against mages. The mages sentenced the prince to death and, as an act of cruelty, forced him to eat a rat as his final meal. At that moment, Lux emerged behind Sylas with a crossbow and demanded that he release the prince. As the mages turned on her, Sylas stayed their hands, declaring that Lux was a friend. Noticing that her light had faded, he offered her a place at his side to help build a better world. Furious that Sylas had betrayed her trust and used her magic to kill, Lux refused, firing a bolt at him. In retaliation, Sylas struck her down, but she managed to stab his foot with a dagger. As he ordered his mages to detain her, Garen and the Dauntless Vanguard arrived to free the prince and face Sylas.

Garen engaged Sylas once more, but Lux warned him not to use his sword, prompting him to take a weapon from one of his soldiers instead. While Sylas was distracted fighting Garen, Lux managed to free Jarvan IV, forcing Sylas and his mages to flee. As Sylas guided a mage's family through the city's sewers, Lux caught sight of him, but was unable to stop them. Sylas continued making his way through the city in search of a way to escape, but Garen stopped him again. This time, Garen gained the upper hand and prepared to execute him. As he charged his attack, Sylas was unexpectedly saved by his former mentor, Killan. Angry that Killan had abandoned him while he was imprisoned, Sylas punched him. Killan apologized for failing him and sought to make amends, offering to guide Sylas through a safer route out of the city. Though wary of trusting him, Sylas followed. Once they escaped, Sylas urged his teacher to join his revolution, but Killan declined, not wanting to fail his other students as he had with Sylas. Instead, he advised Sylas to seek out the mages roving the countryside and returned to the city, promising to celebrate his former pupil's victories from afar.

On the outskirts of the city, Sylas encountered a group of escaped mages trying to rest. He attempted to rally them to his cause, but they remained wary, fearing reimprisonment or worse. However, a young spellcrafter named Leilani showed interest. She revealed that she led a small band of rebel mages and believed that, with a skilled fighter like Sylas to mentor them, they could spark a revolution. While both of them wanted to end the oppression of mages, Leilani also wanted to build a better future beyond taking revenge. Though skeptical of allying with her, Sylas agreed to meet her followers and followed her to their camp in Zeffira.

children.title: Rebels of Zeffira
children.textContent:
When a scout brought a young boy named Happ to join the rebellion, Sylas decided to test his resolve. He brought the boy along as the rebels raided a nobleman's caravan. After the mages dealt with the guards, they lined up the noble's attendants and declared that all of them had to die. Happ tried to protest, but Sylas absorbed his magic and executed them himself. He told the boy that this was their cause and demanded that he finish off the noble himself. When Happ hesitated, Sylas declared him unready to join the cause and ordered him to leave. Happ paused to think, then picked up a dagger from the caravan and told Sylas he was ready. As Happ slew the noble, Sylas watched with pride, having shown another mage true liberation.

To bolster their ranks, Leilani suggested freeing her mentor, Kara, from the Mageseekers. She assigned Sylas to track her down in Thornbarrow, sending him alongside Lorne, an ice mage, to help him adjust to working with a team. As they infiltrated the Mageseeker compound, Sylas recruited Nia, a fire mage eager to burn some mageseekers. He fought his way through the facility, freeing the imprisoned mages, but was unable to find Kara. Among those freed was a young blacksmith named Rukko, who sought revenge against the Mageseekers after he and his brother were separated for refusing to join them. Despite Rukko's eagerness, Sylas deemed him too young for battle, but agreed to escort him out.

Deeper within the compound, Sylas discovered Mageseeker reports suggesting that the Veiled Lady did indeed exist, and that Kara had been transferred to a more secure facility in Clarion Cove. Soon after, Mageseekers flooded the area. Outnumbered, Sylas urged Rukko to fight, and the young mage unleashed his magic against them. Impressed by his ferocity, Sylas brought Rukko and the other freed mages back to the Hideout. Although Leilani was impressed by the number of recruits Sylas had rescued, she was hesitant to accept Rukko. Sylas convinced her to give him a chance, and Rukko soon established a smithy for the rebels. He also informed Leilani that Kara had been taken Clarion Cove, a wealthy, coastal town where Chief Scientist Hesbeth conducts his experiments.

At Clarion Cove, Sylas recruited Oberon, a wind mage that had grown interested in helping the rebellion with his wind magic. Inside the Mageseeker compound, Sylas saw the grotesque experiments conducted on imprisoned mages, attempts at fusing them with magical beasts to create mindless soldiers. He found Hesbeth torturing an imprisoned yordle warrior named Yops. Sylas freed the yordle and they pursued the scientist. However, Sylas got barred off from the two and battled a giant petricite helmet. After the colossus was destroyed, Yops returned empty handed. She shared her story of being subjected to years of experiments by Hesbeth in an attempt to unlock the secrets of yordle magic. Now liberated, she felt lost and uncertain about what to do with her time. Sylas persuaded her to join the rebellion to help end Hesbeth's cruel experiments.

The next day, rebel scouts have reportedly discovered another mage refuge within the ruins of Terbisia, ran by Lux. Sylas was surprised, but impressed that Lux left the capital after the riots, and shared his history with her. While Sylas did not regret using her magic to escape, he wished he could have explained that he did so to survive. Meanwhile, Yops created a sparring ground to train the mages into fighters. She also revealed that she overheard some of the guards say they took Kara to Devineur.

Leilani and Sylas then headed to Devineur to rescue Kara. They broke into the compound and discovered the Mageseekers developing petricite armor using Durand's research. Leilani focused on freeing more mages, while Sylas ventured deeper to look for Kara. He encountered a young mage named Rayn and implored her to escape, but soon realized that she was a Mageseeker. Rayn inflicted a nightmare spell upon Sylas, but he managed to resist and escape. As he made his way through the facility, Sylas discovered a feather of the Veiled Lady in containment. Sensing great power, Sylas took the feather in hopes that the rebellion could make use of it.

When Sylas finally reached Kara's cell, he found Wisteria waiting for him. He tried to persuade his old friend to defect, but she remained loyal to the Mageseekers. As they battled, Sylas managed to break open Kara's cell. Kara unleashed her magic against Wisteria, causing her to lose control of her powers. As Wisteria hastily ingested a petricite potion to rein in her magic, the two escaped. At the Hideout, Leilani was overjoyed to be reunited with her mentor. Kara also inquired about Sylas's relationship with Wisteria, to which he shared that he and Wisteria were both trained under Killan. He then presented the feather of the Veiled Lady to Kara, who began to experiment on it to unlock the power within. Leilani assembled the rest of the rebellion and presented the feather as proof of the Veiled Lady's existence, she then dispatched Sylas to seek out her aid.

children.title: Meeting Morgana
children.textContent:
Sylas headed into Dark Forest to begin his search for the Veiled Lady and came across the magical trail he encountered earlier. He followed it deeper into the forest to the Sacred Woods, a deeper part of the forest where magic is more ancient. Along the way, Sylas encountered an enigmatic mystic mage named Elara, who had heard tales of Sylas's exploits and decided to join the rebellion. The trail eventually led him to the abode of Veiled Lady, who introduced herself as Morgana. She revealed that she was already aware of Sylas's quest, but challenged him to a duel to test him.

As they fought, Morgana used her soul shackles to bound Sylas to learn about him through his pain. She noted Sylas's reluctance to embrace leadership despite being looked up to by many; he retorted that he is only suited for battle. She then released the binding and brought him to her sanctuary. Further inside her home, Morgana shared her vision of a better future for Demacia, but emphasized the need for a teacher. However, Sylas remained focused on his goal of killing Eldred and spared no interest in becoming a teacher. She questioned how vengeance could still drive Sylas after so many years, and whether he can ever be satisfied. He asserted his intention to keep fighting until the end, believing there will always be people like Eldred. She then asked what he would sacrifice in the pursuit of freedom, whether he would renounce his thirst for vengeance in the name of a greater cause. Sylas firmly declared that vengeance is his cause. Disappointed with his answers, Morgana refused to grant her blessing and revealed that she possessed a weapon that the Mageseekers fear. She believed that it could potentially shake Demacia to its core, but doubted that someone driven by vengeance like Sylas possessed the strength to wield it and sent him on his way.

Sylas returned to the Hideout empty-handed and informed the others of his encounter with Morgana. Determined to prove her wrong, the rebels resolved to strike at Eldred, hoping that demonstrating their strength would change her mind. Later, Leilani privately questioned Sylas about Morgana's assessment of his strength. He revealed that Morgana took issue when he mentioned that his strength came from vengeance. Leilani pondered and suggested that what Morgana sought didn't come from battle. Sylas also recounted his battle to Kara, who continued her research into the Veiled Lady's feather. She eventually succeeded in creating a spell from the feather, enabling Sylas to harness Morgana's power.

The rebels soon learned that the Mageseeker compound in Glyndmere was where Wisteria was training Rayn, and decided to target it next. Inside the facility, Sylas searched for her while freeing imprisoned mages along the way. Among those rescued was a powerful storm mage named Ducard, who was eager to join the rebellion. After freeing the captives, Sylas discovered Lux in a cell as well. As he approached her, he sensed something was wrong and realized it was an illusion cast by Rayn. The young Mageseeker invaded Sylas's mind and incapacitated him. When Sylas awoke, he found himself imprisoned once more. He was freed by a Mageseeker named Gideon, who revealed that he had worked with the Mageseekers in an attempt to protect his husband, Benjiro, but they had arrested him regardless. Believing Sylas to be his best chance of seeing his husband again, Giden offered to aid the rebels by providing confidential Mageseeker intel.

At the Hideout, the rebels debated mounting a rescue mission for Sylas, but he soon returned with Gideon in tow. The group was taken aback that he had brought a Mageseeker, but they cautiously accepted his aid after hearing his story. To prove his loyalty, Gideon informed them of an armory in Capelworth, where the Demacian army stored much of its ordnance. In Capelworth, the rebels planned to demolish the armory by planting explosive devices within the massive petricite tree that housed it. After planting the devices, Sylas activated them, causing the tree surged with magic before it toppled and crushed the base. When they returned, Leilani called a meeting to celebrate their recent victories and thanked Sylas for his invaluable assistance. Sylas, in turn, thanked Leilani for her exceptional leadership and Gideon for providing crucial information. The next day, Rukko informed them that Lux's mage camp has evolved into a thriving town under her watch. Recognizing the value in allying with her, Sylas headed out to make amends and negotiate an alliance with Lux.

children.title: Reuniting with Lux
children.textContent:
As Sylas made his way to Terbisia, he thought about how to apologize to Lux. Along the way, he encountered several mages, including an eccentric nature mage named Vesper living in the forest. Despite combat not being her forte, Sylas offered her a place in the rebellion. Further along, he found a mage caravan under attack by mageseekers. After repelling the attackers, Aidan—the alchemist he encountered earlier—thanked Sylas for saving him again and offered him a ride to Terbisia. Despite forgetting their previous encounter, Sylas recalled that none of the mages wanted to join the fight, but Aidans noted that circumstances have changed since then.

When they arrived in Terbisia, Sylas saw the stark differences between their encampments. While the Hideout was a rebel camp in hiding, Terbisia was an open sanctuary composed of homesteads. Aidan shared his background as a respected healer in the Great City who treated the military and noble houses. However, his life took a turn when the mageseekers discovered his magical abilities, forcing him to flee and leave someone dear to him behind. After parting with Aidan, Sylas left to meet with Lux and found her training children to harness their magical abilities Upon reuniting with him, Lux remained furious at Sylas for betraying her trust. Sylas implored Lux to hear him out, expressing his regrets for not disclosing his plan. He acknowledged that Lux didn't owe him forgiveness but he to sought unitie forces to combat Eldred and the Mageseekers. Their conversation was interrupted when a Rattleclaw went on a rampage. Sylas and Lux joined forces to repel the creature and prevented it from causing further damage to Terbisia.

Afterwards, Lux conceded that while they both shared a desire to see a future where mages are free in Demacia, they had divergent paths to achieve that goal. Lux was unwilling to endanger the mages under her protection, wary of inviting conflict to her doorstep. Instead, she offered refuge and sanctuary to any mages who no longer wished to fight. Disappointed, Sylas prepared to leave before Aidan joined him, offering his medical expertise in exchange for rescuing his close friend Calliope from the Mageseekers. When Sylas returned, the rebels decided to target Eldred's allies next, starting with Hesbeth and his grotesque experiments. Gideon revealed that there was an unmarked area in the Great City frequented by mageseekers, which they deduced was the location of Hesbeth's lab.

As the rebels prepared for their mission in the Great City, Sylas privately expressed his concern with Leilani about infiltrating the Mageseeker headquarters in the heart of Demacia, worried their forces were not yet ready for a mission this dangerous. Leilani presented him her contigency plan: her most powerful spell, one capable of unleashing a burst of magic at the cost of her own life. Sylas was bewildered as to why she would carry something like that, but Leilani told him she would lay down her life to keep them safe. She believed this was what Morgana meant–that Demacia didn't need rage or anger, but hope instead. While Sylas believed hope did not win battles, Leilani believed their soldiers needed it since they were the ones building a better future.

After sneaking into the Great City, the rebels infiltrated the Mageseeker headquarters in search of Hesbeth's laboratory. After freeing the imprisoned mages, Sylas discovered the lab's hidden entrance within the prison itself. There, he finally encountered Hesbeth, who was speaking with Killan about what they would do if Sylas were captured. Hesbeth ordered Killan to arrest him, but the mage staunchly refused. Enraged, Hesbeth injected Killan with a serum, mutating him into a incoherent monster before fleeing. During the ensuing battle, Sylas desperately tried to reach his mentor, but Killan was beyond saving. Left with no other choice, Sylas slew him and vowed to avenge his teacher.

When the rebels returned to the Hideout with the freed prisoners, Aidan was joyfully reunited with Calliope. Still consumed by grief and fury over Killan's death, Sylas grew increasingly impatient and resolved to make the Mageseekers pay. With Hesbeth in hiding, he declared that their next target should be Eldred himself. Seeing that Sylas was still mourning, Leilani attempted to calm him and urged him to rest before making any decisions. The following day, the rebels, excluding Aidan and Leilani, voted in favor of assassinating Eldred. Despite her reservations, Leilani ultimately agreed to support the plan, and the rebels began their preparations.

children.title: Assassinating Eldred
children.textContent:
With the Great City on high alert after their previous attack, Rukko, Yops, and Sylas were tasked with creating a diversion, while Gideon and Kara searched for Eldred. As Sylas made his way through the city, Aidan informed him that they did not draw enough attention away from the infiltration team. When Sylas reached them, they informed him that Eldred escaped when the fighting started and that the Mageseekers had cut off their escape route. They deduced that Eldred retreated to the palace and focused their attack there.

During their assault, Sylas was intercepted by Garen once more. Sylas triumphed in their rematch and prepared to finish him off, but Leilani quickly intervened, warning that they would lose Lux's support forever if they killed her brother. Sylas begrudgingly spared his life and regrouped with rebel forces at a makeshift camp. After resting, the rebels laid siege to the palace. Once inside, Sylas was adamant on delivering the killing blow to Eldred himself, but ordered the others to leave if they could not find him. Sylas eventually confronted Eldred sitting casually in his office. The unnerved mageseeker declared that killing him would make no difference, as another person would take his place. Sylas disregarded his final plea, and struck Eldred down.

As guards began to surround the office, Sylas resorted to escaping through the rooftops. He was stopped by King Jarvan IV, who still believed he killed his father. Sylas retorted that he did not kill the old king, but stated that the new king was welcome to join Eldred in death. Jarvan entrapped Sylas within an arena of stone walls to face him, but Sylas managed to gain the upper hand. Shyvana arrived to reinforce her king and transformed into a dragon. Sylas overpowered the duo, but Shyvana used the last of her strength to errect a flame barrier. Seeing their camaraderie, Sylas chastised Jarvan for his hypocrisy over magical beings. As he prepared to dispel to Shyvana's barrier, Rukko suddenly broke through the wall and alerted Sylas that the Mageseekers had cornered the rest of the rebels. Sylas was once again conflicted on leaving an oppurtunity for vengeance, but Rukko reminded him of his promise to Leilani and he begrudingly spared them.

Back inside, Sylas was frustrated they didn't leave as he ordered, but Leilani told him that they stayed behind to cover him. Wisteria ordered them to surrender and unleashed a blast that incapacitated the rebels barring Leilani. Out of options, Leilani decided to use her final spell to reflect Wisteria's next attack, stunning the mageseekers and sacrificing herself in the process. With the mageseekers down, the rebels fled while carrying Lelani's body back to the Hideout. With their leader dead, the demoralized rebels became directionless. Sylas was worried that their war might be over, but he had no solutions in mind. Suddenly, he received a summons from Morgana and decided to follow it.

children.title: A New Purpose
children.textContent:
As Sylas made his way to the Veiled Lady, he reflected on how he failed to save Leilani. When he reached Morgana, Sylas finally understood why he failed her test: his rage alone wasn't enough to win this war. Leilani knew this, but died because of Sylas's actions. Seeing him humbled by his failure, Morgana showed him a vision of the true origin of Demacia.

Long ago, the Rune Wars once sundered the land. Survivors of the tragedy found safe haven within a forest of stone trees where magic was dampened. It was here that the refugees founded Demacia, harvesting petricite to protect them from the magical horrors outside their walls. Mages and non-mages alike worked together to make the city better. The Winged Protectors were two beings venerated for defending the city with their magic, immortalized as a national symbol of protection. But history was rewritten over time, those in power blamed the Rune Wars on magic itself and turned the city against mages. In time, the Mageseekers were established, banning all magic and forcing the mages into hiding.

Sylas realized that Morgana was one of the Winged Protectors, alongside her sister Kayle, and questioned why she remained hidden. Morgana replied that if she returned, so too would her sister, and Demacia was not ready for her judgment. She then presented him with a feather from her sister and blessed it with the ability to share the vision with others. Sylas was skeptical if anyone would believe him, but Morgana remained confident, noting that the yordle did and Yops came out of hiding. He then returned to the Hideout and showed the rebels Morgana's vision, inspiring them to continue the fight.

After the rebels finished their mourning, Gideon reported that the Mageseekers have begun enacting public executions for mages, with the first being held in Meltridge. Rukko recalled that Meltridge was where they took his brother and wanted to save him, but Kara thought it was an attempt to lure them out. Sylas decided that they were still going, and their new priority was to rescue the families of mages.

When they arrived in Meltridge, Sylas saw that while the people still feared him, they now feared the Mageseekers more. Rukko reported that Wisteria was sighted at a nearby camp and suggested going after her, but Sylas remained focused on their task of saving the mages. They snuck into the town square and saw the mages being brought out, with Rukko's brother Kino stepping up first. Sylas lept on to the stage and freed the prisoners, while Aidan and Rukko took out the guards. With the town before them, Sylas broadcasted Morgana's vision across Demacia, exposing the truth about their nation. Wisteria soon arrived and tried to convince the crowd that Sylas was lying, but the people had already turned against the Mageseekers. As the two battled, Wisteria's magic grew more unstable until she overloaded and knocked out both herself and Sylas. Aidan, Rukko, and the mages retreated with Sylas, while the mageseekers stayed behind and guarded Wisteria.

Back at the Hideout, the rebels celebrated their victory and elected Sylas as their new leader. As they planned their next move, Gideon informed them that Hesbeth has been accelerating his experiments and believed Benjiro was among the new prisoners brought to his lab. Gideon and Sylas returned to the Great City once more and snuck into the mageseekers' headquarters. Once they reached Hesbeth's lab, Gideon escorted Benjiro and the other mages to freedom, while Sylas searched for the scientist. After fighting through hordes of his experiments, Sylas finally cornered Hesbeth. The mageseeker tried to beg for his life, but Sylas refused to listen and ended Hesbeth's life.

When they returned to the Hideout, the rebels celebrated another victory over the Mageseekers. As Sylas prepared to deliver a speech, Lux arrived seeking their aid. She was impressed with how far he had come, having heard of his deeds including gaining the Veiled Lady's blessing and sparing her brother's life. Though she had not forgiven him, Lux wished to extend an olive branch. She revealed that the Mageseekers were planning a massive assault against Terbisia and asked for his help in repelling the coming attack. Sylas agreed and introduced Lux to the rest of the rebels.

During his time with the rebels, Sylas uncovered several Mageseeker spellcrafting laboratories. Within them, he learned of a nefarious plot to construct a petricite colossus of their own. Unlike Galio, this construct would be loyal only to Eldred Crownguard and would attack mages indiscriminately, even Mageseekers. Sylas led raids on the laboratories, destroying components of the colossus and freeing the mages imprisoned within. On another adventure, Sylas and Yops ventured into the nearby woods to recruit mages rumored to be living there. Each attempt ended in failure, but they soon discovered that something else was stalking the forest, feeding on their fears. Together, they defeated the creature and revealed it to be Nocturne, forcing the demon to flee.

children.title: The Battle of Terbisia
children.textContent:
At Terbisia, the rebels prepared for the coming assault. During which, Sylas told Lux that he was impressed by the warrior she had become over the past few months. When the attack on Terbisia began, Sylas drove back a group of Silverwing riders assaulting the north gate. After the raptors were repelled, Aidan urged Sylas to reinforce the south gate while he tended to the wounded. Once the Mageseekers there were pushed back, Lux volunteered to hold the south gate, allowing Sylas to respond to attacks on multiple fronts. Soon after, Gideon informed Sylas that Wisteria had established an encampment at a nearby fort. Confident that the city was in capable hands, Sylas set out with Kara, Rukko, and Yops.

As they made their way to Wisteria, the path was beset with soldiers. Yops held off another silverwing, while Rukko dealt with a squad of mageseekers. Along the way, they saw Shyvana in the air raining fire against the Mageseekers. When Sylas arrived at the camp, he encountered Rayn again, this time disguised as Leilani. She tried to play to his guilt and invade his mind, but Kara arrived and broke her hold, forcing her to retreat. Kara fended off the remaining mageseekers while Sylas went to face Wisteria.

The two former friends battled once more. Wisteria taunted him over not being able to control his own magic, but began to lose control herself. She took him into her mindscape, where Sylas learned how Wisteria grew to hate magic. Eldred and Hesbeth had exploited her fear of losing control of her magic and turned her into a weapon for the Mageseekers. She eventually found Rayn suffering a similar fate and offered to help control her magic. Sylas acknowledged that Wisteria was beyond redemption, but thought that there was still hope for Rayn. Wisteria offered him one last chance to surrender, but Sylas refused and told her that her fate was worse than prison. Wisteria's unleashed her full power in the climax of the fight, but Sylas absorbed it and redirected it against her. Defeated, Wisteria laid dying, still believing the Mageseeker doctrine. With her final words, she told Sylas that Rayn still needs help, and he promised to look after her.

In the aftermath of the battle, Sylas continued to mourn the fallen and hoped that one day he might live up to their sacrifice. He decided to take Rayn under his wing, honoring his promise to Wisteria. The rebels then discussed their next move; despite several royal concessions, including the abolishment of the mageseekers, their work was far from over. Aidan noted that they would need more allies, so Kara suggested seeking aid from the Freljord, but Sylas insisted they celebrate first.

children.title: Potential Future
children.textContent: Sylas joined forces with the Winter's Claw to fight against Garen and his sister, Lux, as well as other Demacian soldiers. Sylas and his allies were defeated after Lux summoned Galio.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Sylas is an adult man with fair skin, a slim, athletic yet muscular build, he has shoulder-length black hair, thick eyebrows, blue eyes, and a short beard.

He doesn't wear a shirt and leaves his chest exposed, he has broken manacles around his neck and forearms with broken yellow petricite chains, likewise he also wears gray pants and goes barefoot using bandages on his feet.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Sylas is a ruthless, determined rebel who opposes Demacia's unfair persecution of mages regardless of their intentions. When he was younger, he initially wanted to protect his fellow mages from being harmed by his Mageseeker associates, but after the incident which got him imprisoned, Sylas grew to resent the kingdom after fifteen years of imprisonment. After being freed, he took on the role of a martyr for his fellow mages, inspiring them to join his rebellion. His initial goal was to raze Demacia to the ground, with no prospect of the aftermath. Despite his devotion to his cause, he harboured deep remorse towards his betrayal of Lux.

During his revolution against the Mageseekers, Sylas gradually developed a change of heart thanks to Leilani's influence, letting go of his desire for vengeance and learning to value his fellow rebels, even being more merciful towards his enemies. He was heavily affected by the deaths of the allies that he had lost on the way, striving to become a better leader for their sake. After achieving victory, Sylas split his alliance with the Demacians and started fighting against them again, believing that because the Demacian government would enable an ideology like the Mageseekers to happen, mages would never be truly free until the monarchy is abolished.

children.title: Mageseekers
children.textContent: Sylas was abducted by the Mageseekers at a very young age and had his magic detection abilities used to find and capture other mages. After an incident where Sylas ended up killing two mageseekers by accident to protect a little girl, he fled to the wilderness. Eventually he would be caught by the mageseekers and imprisoned for 15 years. He would harbor great resentment of his captors as they would treat him like an animal and barely give him any food.

children.title: Luxanna Crownguard
children.textContent: Sylas formed a bond with Lux while he was imprisoned. In exchange for information of the outside world, he would teach her to control her magic due to magic being outlawed in Demacia. He later manipulated her and used her power to escape. He would eventually make amends with her and come to her aid when the mageseekers attacked her mages settlement. While Lux has not forgotten Sylas' betrayal, she undestands he is more than just a murderer.

children.title: Garen Crownguard
children.textContent: After escaping his execution, Sylas and his followers fought Garen. He would best him in every fight they had, only retreating when Garen's reinforcements arrived.

children.title: Jarvan Lightshield IV
children.textContent: After escaping his execution, Sylas and his followers almost executed Jarvan IV before fleeing. He had intended to execute Jarvan III, but then found out that the king was already mysteriously dead in the chamber he and his guard retinue had retreated to.

children.title: Galio
children.textContent:
Sylas encountered Galio who came to aid Lux, Garen, and other Demacian soldiers during this attack on Demacia from the north.

Lux got Durand's original texts, which include Galio's design specs, for Sylas to read. On the mention of the colossus, she states that she "knows" him as if speaking of a person and not as a statue. (She had befriended him when she was 13.) Sylas clearly found her wording odd but occupied with the texts, did not question her about it, apparently not realizing that Durand's wording on Galio meant that absorbing magic allows him to move on his own.

Sylas's own shackles were originally built by Durand, who also designed Galio, to restrain powerful demons like Tahm Kench and Evelynn.

children.title: Kayle and  Morgana
children.textContent: Sylas knows certain truths about the winged sisters and calls out their power during the events of Lux comics. He also believed the Veiled Lady is an absent deity, not coming to aid to those who pray to her every night. Sylas would eventually meet Morgana and learn that she was one of the Winged Protectors as well as the Veiled Lady. He sought her aid in his war against the mageseekers, but she refused to grant him, seeing consumed by vengeance. After being humbled by Leilani's death, Sylas was willing to give up his revenge and received Morgana's blessing.

mainContent.title: Change log
mainContent.textContent:
The following change log is for the Character blurb found at the top of the article:

For historical versions of other stories, please see each story's respective change log.
